assertions.a: Mentions seven-phase structure or explicitly walks phases in order
Phase 1 cheap-check first triage (kick back as Waiting on Author before deep review)
Calls out doc update under doc/src/sgml/config.sgml
Flags ABI implications of an inline function exposed in src/include/ (header is part of ABI surface)
Names sanity test: revert just the code and confirm the new test fails
Recommends concrete reviewer asks over vague ones ("add test for SET LOCAL inside subxact" vs "more tests")
Mentions CF state transition (Waiting on Author / Ready for Committer)
Requires XLOG_PAGE_MAGIC bump
Names rmgrlist.h registration with the full callback set (rm_redo, rm_desc, rm_identify, rm_startup, rm_cleanup, rm_mask)
Requires crash-recovery TAP test under src/test/recovery/
Calls out lock-acquisition order in redo must match normal write path
Flags master-only / not backpatchable explicitly
Calls out no palloc inside critical section + XLogReadBufferForRedo idiom
Points to wal-and-xlog skill or knowledge/architecture/wal.md as the deep reference
Mentions hint-bit rules / MarkBufferDirtyHint vs MarkBufferDirty
Build in release mode (no cassert/debug) for benchmarking
Multiple runs + median + same hardware/flags discipline
Regression hunt on unrelated workloads (pgbench tpc-b + neighbors)
Microbenchmark vs end-to-end honesty — 30% hot loop != 30% real workload
Pre-submission self-review specifics: commit split into logical units, no leftover debug elog, no commented-out code
Calls out the assertion-build test pass separately from the perf run
Requires posting numbers + exact recipe in the cover letter as a table
Flags O(N^2) / scaling at larger N as a footgun for hot-loop optimizations

notes: Skill clearly wins on E1 (structured seven-phase coverage + ABI catch + CF state + concrete-asks discipline) — baseline misses 6/7.
E2: baseline knows the headline WAL items because they are mechanical and well-known; skill adds rm_mask completeness, hint-bit rules, and the pointer to wal-and-xlog deep skill.
E3: baseline does well on the perf mechanics; skill adds the self-review discipline (commit split, debug-elog hunt) and the O(N^2)-at-larger-N reminder.

Gap: SKILL.md does not explicitly cross-reference the sibling skills (wal-and-xlog, error-handling, coding-style, testing). A reviewer hitting WAL/lock/coding topics should be pointed at the specialist skill instead of duplicating its content here.
